examples.title: First Fit Allocation Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm to allocate memory using First Fit strategy
examples.explanation: First Fit searches memory from the beginning and allocates the first hole that is large enough. It then updates the hole list by removing the allocated block and adding back any remaining space.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of holes

examples.title: Best Fit Allocation Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm to allocate memory using Best Fit strategy
examples.code:
// Best Fit Memory Allocation
// holes: list of available memory blocks
// processSize: size of memory requested

FUNCTION BestFit(holes, processSize):
 bestHole = NULL
 minSize = INFINITY
 
 FOR each hole IN holes:
 IF hole.size >= processSize THEN
 IF hole.size < minSize THEN
 minSize = hole.size
 bestHole = hole
 END IF
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 IF bestHole != NULL THEN
 allocatedAddress = bestHole.start
 remainingSize = bestHole.size - processSize
 
 REMOVE bestHole FROM holes
 
 IF remainingSize > 0 THEN
 newHole = (bestHole.start + processSize, remainingSize)
 ADD newHole TO holes
 END IF
 
 RETURN allocatedAddress
 END IF
 
 RETURN -1 // Out of memory
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: Best Fit searches through all holes to find the smallest one that can accommodate the request. This minimizes wasted space but requires scanning all holes.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for searching, O(n log n) if sorted

examples.title: Paging Address Translation
examples.description: Translation of virtual address to physical address using page table
examples.explanation: This demonstrates how the MMU translates virtual addresses to physical addresses by extracting the page number, looking up the frame in the page table, and combining the frame number with the offset.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) with TLB hit, O(n) with page table lookup

examples.title: FIFO Page Replacement Algorithm
examples.description: Implementation of First In First Out page replacement
examples.code:
// FIFO Page Replacement Algorithm
// frames: array representing physical memory frames
// pages: sequence of page references
// numFrames: number of available frames

FUNCTION FIFO_PageReplacement(pages, numFrames):
 // Initialize frames as empty
 frames = ARRAY of size numFrames
 FOR i = 0 to numFrames-1:
 frames[i] = -1 // Empty
 END FOR
 
 pageFaults = 0
 front = 0 // Points to oldest page
 rear = 0 // Points to next insertion point
 count = 0 // Number of frames filled
 
 FOR each page IN pages:
 // Check if page is already in memory
 IF page NOT IN frames THEN
 pageFaults = pageFaults + 1
 
 IF count < numFrames THEN
 // Frames not full, simply add page
 frames[rear] = page
 rear = (rear + 1) % numFrames
 count = count + 1
 ELSE
 // Frames full, replace oldest (FIFO)
 frames[front] = page
 front = (front + 1) % numFrames
 rear = (rear + 1) % numFrames
 END IF
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 RETURN pageFaults
END FUNCTION

// Example: pages = [1,2,3,4,1,2,5,1,2,3], frames = 3
examples.explanation: FIFO maintains a queue of pages in memory. When a page needs to be replaced, the oldest page (first in queue) is removed. It is simple to implement but may not perform optimally.
examples.timeComplexity: O(f) where f is number of frames

examples.title: LRU Page Replacement Algorithm
examples.description: Implementation of Least Recently Used page replacement using counters
examples.code:
// LRU Page Replacement Algorithm
// Using counter-based implementation

FUNCTION LRU_PageReplacement(pages, numFrames):
 frames = ARRAY of size numFrames
 counters = ARRAY of size numFrames
 time = 0
 pageFaults = 0
 
 // Initialize
 FOR i = 0 to numFrames-1:
 frames[i] = -1
 counters[i] = 0
 END FOR
 
 FOR each page IN pages:
 found = FALSE
 
 // Check if page is in memory
 FOR i = 0 to numFrames-1:
 IF frames[i] == page THEN
 found = TRUE
 counters[i] = time // Update recent use
 BREAK
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 IF NOT found THEN
 pageFaults = pageFaults + 1
 
 // Find LRU page (smallest counter)
 minCounter = INFINITY
 lruIndex = -1
 
 FOR i = 0 to numFrames-1:
 IF counters[i] < minCounter THEN
 minCounter = counters[i]
 lruIndex = i
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 // Replace LRU page
 frames[lruIndex] = page
 counters[lruIndex] = time
 END IF
 
 time = time + 1
 END FOR
 
 RETURN pageFaults
END FUNCTION

// Note: Real implementations use hardware support (reference bits)
examples.explanation: LRU replaces the page that was used least recently. It provides a good approximation to optimal replacement but requires hardware support for accurate implementation.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n × f) where n = pages, f = frames
